title: SEND Survey - April 2026 | Rosie Duffield MP
headings: h3: MP for Canterbury, Whitstable and the Villages
text_excerpt: --> SEND Survey - April 2026 | Rosie Duffield MP top of page Rosie Duffield MP for Canterbury, Whitstable and the Villages HOME ABOUT ROSIE PRIORITIES Defending our Green Spaces A Brighter Future for Our Young Building a Fairer Economy Protecting our Waterways Strengthening our NHS A Stronger Relationship with Europe CONSTITUENCY NEWS SEND Survey - April 2026 Birth Trauma Inquiry CONTACT More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. SEND Survey - April 2026 The Government is planning on introducing new reforms it says will enhance support for children and young people with Special Educational Needs and Disabilities (SEND). You can find out more about the proposals here . I want to hear from you. Your experiences and insights into SEND are incredibly valuable and will play a crucial role in shaping my response. Please take a few minutes to complete this short survey by 12pm on Monday 13th April 2026. First name * Last name Email * Postcode * I am completing this survey because I am a... * Parent Teacher Medical Professional Service Provider Other Which education setting are you experienced in? * Mainstream Specialist Alternative Provision Early Years Post-16 Education Other 1. How would you describe your current experience of SEND? * 2. What do you think 'excellent' SEND provision looks like? * 3. To what extent do you agree that these proposed changes will positively impact children and their families? * Strongly Agree Agree Neutral Disagree Strongly Disagree 4. To what extent do you agree that mainstream schools will have enough funding and staff to provide extra support under the new system? * Strongly Agree Agree Neutral Disagree Strongly Disagree 5. The government has costed this reforms at £1.6bn for inclusion, £200m for training and £1.8bn to rebuilding local authority SEND teams. Do you think this amount is adequate to tackle the scale of reform required? * 6. Have you seen excellent SEND support in a Kent school that you think should be a model for others nationwide? * 7. What would you like decision makers to know about the needs of children requiring extra support? * 8. Is there anything else you'd like me to know about your experience of SEND? * If you do not wish to receive our newsletter, please tick this box. Submit Feedback STAY IN TOUCH! Sign up for Rosie's Newsletter SUBSCRIBE Thanks for submitting! Contact Rosie's Team Address House of Commons, London, SW1A 0AA Email enquiries@rosieduffieldmp.co.uk Keeping you posted Don't forget to stay in touch by signing up for Rosie's Newsletter email using the form above, and by following Rosie on social media at the accounts below.
